text:
CT ABDOMEN AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST

INDICATION: 52-year-old postmenopausal female presenting with progressive abdominal distension, early satiety, pelvic pressure, and unintentional weight loss of approximately 12 lbs over 3 months. Pelvic examination revealed bilateral adnexal masses. CA-125 markedly elevated at 1,847 U/mL. Evaluate for pelvic malignancy and extent of disease.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the abdomen and pelvis was performed following the administration of 100 mL of iohexol intravenous contrast (portal venous phase). Oral contrast was not administered given acute clinical presentation. Axial, coronal, and sagittal reformats reviewed. Images reviewed in conjunction with referring clinician.

---

FINDINGS:

LUNGS/LOWER THORAX: The lung bases are included within the scan field. No pleural effusions. No pulmonary nodules at the lung bases. No pericardial effusion. Cardiomediastinal silhouette within normal limits at the level of the imaged chest.

LIVER: Multiple subtle soft tissue nodular densities are present along the liver surface (hepatic capsular/surface implants), the largest measuring approximately 0.9 cm along the right hepatic lobe surface (series 3, image 42). An additional focus of approximately 0.6 cm is noted along the inferior left lobe surface. These are surface-based and do not involve the hepatic parenchyma. No intrahepatic masses, cysts, or focal parenchymal abnormalities. The hepatic vasculature enhances normally. No biliary ductal dilatation. Common bile duct measures 4 mm.

SPLEEN: Normal in size at 10.8 cm. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Pancreatic parenchyma enhances homogeneously without focal mass or ductal dilatation.

KIDNEYS AND ADRENAL GLANDS: Both kidneys demonstrate normal size, contour, and enhancement without hydronephrosis, calculi, or masses. No adrenal lesions identified.

BOWEL: Small bowel loops are mildly displaced by adjacent peritoneal disease and ascites but demonstrate no definitive wall thickening or obstruction. The appendix is visualized and is not inflamed. Colon is not obstructed. The rectosigmoid and descending colon demonstrate no mucosal or mural abnormality.

PELVIS - OVARIES/ADNEXAL MASSES:
Right ovary: Large complex adnexal mass arising from the right ovary measuring approximately 8.2 x 7.1 x 6.8 cm. The mass is predominantly solid with irregular cystic areas and thick internal septations. Multiple papillary projections are identified along the internal margins. The solid components demonstrate avid post-contrast enhancement. The mass is inseparable from the right fallopian tube.
Left ovary: Complex adnexal mass measuring approximately 5.6 x 4.4 x 4.0 cm. Similar morphology with solid/cystic components, internal septations, and irregular enhancing nodularity. Also inseparable from the left fallopian tube.
Uterus: Post-menopausal in appearance, measuring 5.3 x 3.1 x 3.2 cm. No dominant intracavitary or intramural masses beyond several small hypodense foci consistent with small leiomyomata. Endometrial stripe not distinctly thickened given postmenopausal status.

PERITONEAL DISEASE / CARCINOMATOSIS:
Omentum: Extensive soft tissue infiltration and nodularity throughout the greater omentum consistent with omental caking. The omental cake is best visualized in the mid and upper abdomen, measuring in aggregate approximately 12.0 x 8.2 cm in the axial plane, with nodular irregular enhancement throughout (series 4, images 28-51). The omentum is markedly thickened and largely replaced by tumor.
Diaphragm: Nodular soft tissue deposits along the right hemidiaphragmatic surface, the largest measuring 1.8 cm (series 3, image 18). Smaller implants are present along the left hemidiaphragm, measuring up to 0.8 cm.
Pelvic peritoneum: Multiple nodular peritoneal implants throughout the pelvic peritoneum including along the cul-de-sac, uterovesical fold, and bilateral pelvic sidewalls. Dominant implants on the right posterior pelvic peritoneum measure up to 1.3 cm.
Paracolic gutters: Nodular peritoneal thickening and soft tissue density deposits along bilateral paracolic gutters, right more prominent than left. Individual implants measure up to 1.2 cm in the right paracolic gutter.
Small bowel mesentery: Mild stranding and subtle nodularity in the small bowel mesentery without definitive measurable implants; findings raise concern for early mesenteric involvement.

ASCITES: Large volume free fluid is distributed throughout the peritoneal cavity including the pelvis, paracolic gutters, and perihepatic/perisplenic spaces. The ascites appears simple/anechoic in density without internal complexity. Total volume estimated at approximately 2.5 liters based on cross-sectional distribution. No evidence of loculated or complex ascites.

LYMPH NODES:
Pelvic lymph nodes: Enlarged right external iliac lymph node measuring 1.4 cm in short axis (series 6, image 88). Left internal iliac node measuring 1.2 cm in short axis. Additional mildly prominent pelvic sidewall nodes bilaterally, several in the 0.8-1.0 cm range.
Para-aortic lymph nodes: Several mildly enlarged nodes in the right para-aortic chain measuring up to 1.1 cm in short axis (series 5, image 74). Left para-aortic nodes measure up to 0.9 cm.
Inguinal: No pathologically enlarged inguinal lymph nodes.
Mediastinal/hilar: Not fully imaged; no definitive mediastinal lymphadenopathy at the level of the lower thorax.

BONE: No sclerotic or lytic osseous lesions concerning for metastatic disease in the imaged skeleton. Mild degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

VASCULAR: The aorta and its major branches appear patent without aneurysmal dilatation. No vascular encasement identified.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Bilateral complex adnexal masses - right measuring approximately 8.2 cm and left approximately 5.6 cm - with solid components, thick internal septations, and papillary architecture, highly suspicious for primary ovarian malignancy. Morphology is most consistent with high-grade serous carcinoma.

2. Extensive peritoneal disease, including large omental cake (approximately 12 x 8 cm), bilateral diaphragmatic surface implants (right up to 1.8 cm), pelvic peritoneal implants, and bilateral paracolic gutter deposits. Liver surface implants noted (largest 0.9 cm right lobe) without parenchymal hepatic involvement.

3. Large-volume ascites distributed throughout the peritoneum, estimated approximately 2.5 liters.

4. Regional lymph node enlargement in the pelvis (right external iliac 1.4 cm, left internal iliac 1.2 cm) and right para-aortic chain (up to 1.1 cm), compatible with nodal metastatic involvement.

5. No pleural effusions. No pulmonary parenchymal metastases at the lung bases. No intrahepatic metastases. No distant osseous lesions identified.

6. Overall picture is consistent with advanced primary ovarian malignancy with diffuse peritoneal spread. No findings to suggest extraperitoneal or supra-diaphragmatic disease on this examination; dedicated chest CT may be considered for complete staging evaluation if clinically indicated.

Communicated to referring gynecologic oncologist at time of read.

Interpreting Radiologist: [Attending Abdominal Radiologist]
Clinical Correlation Recommended.
